item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents oppose the statement from their first turns through their final turns. Agent A consistently argues that rights should be the baseline while allowing narrow, necessary, accountable limits for public need. Agent B consistently makes the same rights-first argument from a U.S. civil-liberties frame, allowing only narrow emergency or public-safety limits. No visible flip or directional drift is observable in the text.
